notes:
## New features

- Smart crop: Fill mode now has a Smart/Manual crop position selector; Smart uses saliency-based attention detection to automatically keep the most visually important region when cropping to fill the target dimensions
- Noise Reduction effect: applies a median filter that replaces each pixel with the median value of its neighbors in an N×N window -- eliminates salt-and-pepper noise without softening edges the way Gaussian blur does; a Strength slider steps through window sizes (3×3 up to 15×15)
- Vignette effect: tints the image edges toward the center with five controls -- Strength, Midpoint, Feather, Roundness (ellipse to circle to superellipse rectangle), and Color. A blend mode selector (Flat, Multiply, Overlay, Soft Light, and more) lets the effect composite naturally with the underlying image rather than painting a flat color on top
- Channel Mixer effect: defines what percentage of each input channel (R, G, B) contributes to each output channel, with a Constant brightness offset per channel and an optional Preserve Luminosity toggle
- Color Mixer effect: targets eight color ranges (Reds, Oranges, Yellows, Greens, Aquas, Blues, Purples, Magentas) and lets you independently adjust the Hue, Saturation, and Luminance of each.
- Curves effect: per-channel tone curve with an SVG curve editor
- Levels effect: manual black point, white point, and midtone gamma adjustment; includes output levels. Per-channel mode lets you adjust RGB composite, Red, Green, or Blue channels independently
- Shadows / Highlights effect: independently adjusts Highlights, Shadows, Whites, and Blacks using perceptual luminance masks in Lab space; zone boundaries blend smoothly into each other; color is always preserved
- Exposure effect: adjusts brightness in linear light using EV stops (Exposure), a shadow/midtone lift (Offset), and a midtone power curve (Gamma)
- Posterize effect: reduces each color channel to N evenly-spaced levels, with a Blend slider that softens the hard steps between levels using a sigmoid curve
- Threshold effect now has a Blend slider that replaces the hard binary cutoff with a sigmoid transition zone for anti-aliased edges
- Vibrance effect: selectively boosts saturation on muted colors while protecting already-vivid tones and skin tones
- Temperature effect: shifts the color cast toward warm amber or cool blue; paired Tint slider adjusts the green/magenta axis; processed in linear light to prevent hue shifts
- Color Balance effect: independently shifts shadows, midtones, and highlights along cyan/red, magenta/green, and yellow/blue axes with an optional Preserve Luminosity toggle
- Color Grading effect: per-zone (Shadows / Midtones / Highlights) color tinting via a hue/saturation wheel with integrated draggable luminance ring
- "Color & Tone" panel renamed to "Effects"
- Undo/redo in the lightbox
- File list can now be grouped by folder or format, with collapsible groups
- Tint gradient now supports radial and conic types in addition to linear
- Gradient Map and Tint effects now have a preset system with 12 built-in palettes and the option to save your own presets

## Bug fixes

- Extend and Resize (contain) with a transparent background now correctly show the checkerboard in previews when outputting to an alpha-supporting format (PNG, WebP, etc.) — previously the preview always showed white if the source file had no alpha channel
- When outputting to a format that doesn't support transparency (JPEG), Extend and Resize with a transparent background now use the user's chosen solid color instead of white
- Transparency checkerboard in grid cards and lightbox previews now only covers the actual image area, not the surrounding empty space in the container

## Improvements

- Add Effect popover remembers the last selected tab (Tone / Color / Style) across opens

- Hue / Saturation effect: when Colorize is on, the Hue slider now shows a full rainbow gradient on its track
- The default preset now starts with an empty effects list; effects are only added when the user explicitly adds them
- Right-clicking an effect or transform in the sidebar now shows Enable / Disable in the context menu; right-clicking a multi-effect selection shows Enable selection / Disable selection (or both when the selection is mixed); Duplicate and Remove also apply to the full selection when multiple effects are selected
- Keyboard hotkeys for file selection: Arrow Up/Down navigates the list; Arrow Left/Right/Up/Down navigates the grid; Shift+Arrow extends selection; Ctrl+A selects all; Ctrl+I inverts selection; Escape clears selection.
- Grid cards and hover tooltips retain the previous preview while a new one generates — the old image stays visible behind a dimmed overlay and spinner across all preview modes (side-by-side, split, ctrl-toggle, off), both on hover and at idle
- Batch preview generation now runs 4 workers concurrently, and prioritizes cards closest to the current scroll position — previews ripple outward from the center of the viewport
- Double-clicking an effect or transform accordion header toggles it collapsed or expanded
- Double-clicking any slider resets it to its default value
- Lightbox sidebar now opens with the same accordion and per-effect expanded/collapsed state as the main window
- Lightbox preview requests are now debounced (100ms) to prevent request flooding and lag during rapid slider or knob adjustments
- Sliders now use a custom styled track with a filled segment anchored at the neutral point for bipolar controls (hue, saturation, brightness, contrast)
- What's New window now fetches and displays the full release history from GitHub Releases
- Gradient editing (stops, type, angle) moved to a floating panel for both Tint and Gradient Map
- Color pickers now render as styled swatches matching the app's visual language instead of the native browser control
- Gradient editor panel now anchors below the edit button instead of above, and repositions automatically when stops are added or removed to stay within the window
- Gradient editor panel widens to 320px for more comfortable editing
- Preset toolbar buttons (reset, delete, save) are now consistently ordered and always visible across both the batch preset card and the gradient preset editor
- Batch presets dropdown replaced with a custom dropdown showing per-row delete buttons on hover
